summary: The agent concludes that there is no known relationship between serum paraoxonase levels and epicardial fat tissue thickness because existing medical literature does not support such a link. This conclusion stems from the lack of direct evidence connecting paraoxonase—typically associated with oxidation-related pathways—to measures of epicardial fat volume.
